title: AI is changing how small online sellers decide what to make
description: For years Mike McClary sold the Guardian LTE Flashlight, a heavy-duty black model, online through his small outdoor brand. The product, designed for brightness and durability, became one of his most popular items ever. Even after he stopped offering it around 2017, customers kept sending him emails

title: The Ridiculously Nerdy Intel Bet That Could Rake in Billions
description: Advanced chip packaging is suddenly at the center of the AI boom. Intel is going all in.

title: Copilot is ‘for entertainment purposes only,’ according to Microsoft’s terms of use
description: AI skeptics aren’t the only ones warning users not to unthinkingly trust models’ outputs — that’s what the AI companies say themselves in their terms of service.

title: Can orbital data centers help justify a massive valuation for SpaceX?
description: On the latest episode of TechCrunch’s Equity podcast, we debated Elon Musk's vision for data centers in space.

title: In Japan, the robot isn’t coming for your job; it’s filling the one nobody wants
description: Driven by labor shortages, Japan is pushing physical AI from pilot projects into real-world deployment.

title: CBP facility codes sure seem to have leaked via online flashcards
description: Quizlet flashcards seem to include sensitive information about gate security at CBP locations.

title: Artemis II is going so well that we're left to talk about frozen urine
description: "I think the fixation on the toilet is kind of human nature."

title: Tech companies are trying to neuter Colorado’s landmark right-to-repair law
description: A state bill is a glimpse of how corporations are limiting people's ability to make their own fixes and upgrades.

title: Anthropic says Claude Code subscribers will need to pay extra for OpenClaw usage
description: It’s about to become more expensive for Claude Code subscribers to use Anthropic’s coding assistant with OpenClaw and other third-party tools.

title: Hackers Are Posting the Claude Code Leak With Bonus Malware
description: Plus: The FBI says a recent hack of its wiretap tools poses a national security risk, attackers stole Cisco source code as part of an ongoing supply chain hacking spree, and more.

title: Anthropic is having a moment in the private markets; SpaceX could spoil the party
description: Glen Anderson, president of Rainmaker Securities, says the secondary market for private shares has never been more active — with Anthropic the hottest trade around, OpenAI losing ground, and SpaceX's looming IPO poised to reshape the landscape for everyone.

title: Trump proposes steep cut to NASA budget as astronauts head for the Moon
description: Congress will likely reject the White House's NASA cuts, just as it did last year.

title: Ice Age dice show early Native Americans may have understood probability
description: Ice Age hunter-gatherer "were intentionally relying on random outcomes in repeatable, rule-based ways."

title: Meta Pauses Work With Mercor After Data Breach Puts AI Industry Secrets at Risk
description: Major AI labs are investigating a security incident that impacted Mercor, a leading data vendor. The incident could have exposed key data about how they train AI models.

title: OpenAI’s Fidji Simo Is Taking Medical Leave Amid an Executive Shake-Up
description: The company is undergoing major leadership restructuring as its CEO of AGI deployment goes on leave for “several weeks.”

title: Four things we’d need to put data centers in space
description: MIT Technology Review Explains: Let our writers untangle the complex, messy world of technology to help you understand what’s coming next. You can read more from the series here. title: The 3 Best Portable Jump Starters in 2026: Get Charged Up
description: The new crop of portable jump starters means you'll never be stuck waiting on a tow. Here are the devices we'd trust.

title: The Download: plastic’s problem with fuel prices, and SpaceX’s blockbuster IPO

title: Fuel prices are soaring. Plastic could be next.
description: As the war in Iran continues to engulf the Middle East and the Strait of Hormuz stays closed, one of the most visible global economic ripple effects has been fossil-fuel prices. title: Understanding Convolutions on Graphs
description: Understanding the building blocks and design choices of graph neural networks.

title: A Gentle Introduction to Graph Neural Networks
description: What components are needed for building learning algorithms that leverage the structure and properties of graphs?

title: Distill Hiatus
description: After five years, Distill will be taking a break.

title: Adversarial Reprogramming of Neural Cellular Automata
description: Reprogramming Neural CA to exhibit novel behaviour, using adversarial attacks.

title: Weight Banding
description: Weights in the final layer of common visual models appear as horizontal bands. We investigate how and why.
